mn62:1.2: At one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Sāvatthī, in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s park.
mn62:10.1: And what, Rāhula, is the fire element?
mn62:10.2: The fire element may be internal, or it may be external.
mn62:10.3: And what, Rāhula, is the internal fire element?
mn62:10.4: Whatever is internal, personal, fire, fiery, and appropriated—namely:
mn62:10.5: that by which one is warmed, by which one ages, by which one is burned up, and by which what is eaten, drunk, chewed, and tasted is properly digested—or whatever else there is internally, personal, fire, fiery, and appropriated—
mn62:10.6: this is called the internal fire element, Rāhula.
mn62:10.7: And whatever is the internal fire element and whatever is the external fire element—they are just the fire element.
mn62:10.8: That should be seen as it actually is with proper discernment [*sammappaññāya*] thus: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
mn62:10.9: Having seen this as it actually is with proper discernment [*sammappaññāya*], one becomes disenchanted with the fire element and makes the mind [*citta*] dispassionate toward the fire element.
mn62:11.1: And what, Rāhula, is the air element?
mn62:11.2: The air element may be internal, or it may be external.
mn62:11.3: And what, Rāhula, is the internal air element?
mn62:11.4: Whatever is internal, personal, air, airy, and appropriated—namely:
mn62:11.5: upward-moving winds, downward-moving winds, winds in the belly, winds in the bowels, winds that course through the limbs, in-breath and out-breath—or whatever else there is internally, personal, air, airy, and appropriated—
mn62:11.6: this is called the internal air element, Rāhula.
mn62:11.7: And whatever is the internal air element and whatever is the external air element—they are just the air element.
mn62:11.8: That should be seen as it actually is with proper discernment [*sammappaññāya*] thus: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
mn62:11.9: Having seen this as it actually is with proper discernment [*sammappaññāya*], one becomes disenchanted with the air element and makes the mind [*citta*] dispassionate toward the air element.
mn62:12.1: And what, Rāhula, is the space element?
mn62:12.2: The space element may be internal, or it may be external.
mn62:12.3: And what, Rāhula, is the internal space element?
mn62:12.4: Whatever is internal, personal, space, space-related, and appropriated—namely:
mn62:12.5: the ear-hole, the nostrils, the mouth-opening, that by which what is eaten, drunk, chewed, and tasted is swallowed, where what is eaten, drunk, chewed, and tasted settles, that by which what is eaten, drunk, chewed, and tasted goes out below—or whatever else there is internally, personal, space, space-related, cavity, cavity-related, hollow, unoccupied, appropriated by flesh and blood—
mn62:12.6: this is called the internal space element, Rāhula.
mn62:12.7: And whatever is the internal space element and whatever is the external space element—they are just the space element.
mn62:12.8: That should be seen as it actually is with proper discernment [*sammappaññāya*] thus: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
mn62:12.9: Having seen this as it actually is with proper discernment [*sammappaññāya*], the mind [*citta*] becomes disenchanted with the space element, the mind [*citta*] becomes dispassionate toward the space element.
mn62:13.1: Develop [*bhāvehi*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] that is like earth.
mn62:13.2: For when you develop [*bhāvayato*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] that is like earth, arisen agreeable and disagreeable contacts will not take hold of the mind [*citta*] and remain.
mn62:13.3: Just as, Rāhula, people throw onto the earth what is clean, and what is unclean, and feces, and urine, and spittle, and pus, and blood, yet the earth is not troubled by that, nor ashamed, nor disgusted;
mn62:13.4: even so, Rāhula, develop [*bhāvehi*] the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] that is like earth.
mn62:13.5: For when you develop [*bhāvayato*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] that is like earth, arisen agreeable and disagreeable contacts will not take hold of the mind [*citta*] and remain.
mn62:14.1: Develop [*bhāvehi*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] that is like water.
mn62:14.2: For when you develop [*bhāvayato*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] that is like water, arisen agreeable and disagreeable contacts will not take hold of the mind [*citta*] and remain.
mn62:14.3: Just as, Rāhula, in water people wash what is clean, and what is unclean, and what is smeared with feces, and what is smeared with urine, and what is smeared with spittle, and what is smeared with pus, and what is smeared with blood, yet the water is not troubled by that, nor ashamed, nor disgusted;
mn62:14.4: even so, Rāhula, develop [*bhāvehi*] the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] that is like water.
mn62:14.5: For when you develop [*bhāvayato*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] that is like water, arisen agreeable and disagreeable contacts will not take hold of the mind [*citta*] and remain.
mn62:15.1: Develop [*bhāvehi*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] that is like fire.
mn62:15.2: For when you develop [*bhāvayato*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] that is like fire, arisen agreeable and disagreeable contacts will not take hold of the mind [*citta*] and remain.
mn62:15.3: Just as, Rāhula, fire burns what is clean, and what is unclean, and what is smeared with feces, and what is smeared with urine, and what is smeared with spittle, and what is smeared with pus, and what is smeared with blood, yet the fire is not troubled by that, nor ashamed, nor disgusted;
mn62:15.4: even so, Rāhula, develop [*bhāvehi*] the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] that is like fire.
mn62:15.5: For when you develop [*bhāvayato*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] that is like fire, arisen agreeable and disagreeable contacts will not take hold of the mind [*citta*] and remain.
mn62:16.1: Develop [*bhāvehi*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] that is like air.
mn62:16.2: For when you develop [*bhāvayato*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] that is like air, arisen agreeable and disagreeable contacts will not take hold of the mind [*citta*] and remain.
mn62:16.3: Just as, Rāhula, air blows over what is clean, and what is unclean, and what is smeared with feces, and what is smeared with urine, and what is smeared with spittle, and what is smeared with pus, and what is smeared with blood, yet the air is not troubled by that, nor ashamed, nor disgusted;
mn62:16.4: even so, Rāhula, develop [*bhāvehi*] the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] that is like air.
mn62:16.5: For when you develop [*bhāvayato*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] that is like air, arisen agreeable and disagreeable contacts will not take hold of the mind [*citta*] and remain.
mn62:17.1: Develop [*bhāvehi*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] that is like space.
mn62:17.2: For when you develop [*bhāvayato*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] that is like space, arisen agreeable and disagreeable contacts will not take hold of the mind [*citta*] and remain.
mn62:17.3: Just as, Rāhula, space is not established anywhere;
mn62:17.4: even so, Rāhula, develop [*bhāvehi*] the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] that is like space.
mn62:17.5: For when you develop [*bhāvayato*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] that is like space, arisen agreeable and disagreeable contacts will not take hold of the mind [*citta*] and remain.
mn62:18.1: Develop [*bhāvehi*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] of goodwill [*mettā*].
mn62:18.2: For when you develop [*bhāvayato*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] of goodwill [*mettā*], whatever ill will there is will be abandoned.
mn62:19.1: Develop [*bhāvehi*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] of compassion [*karuṇā*].
mn62:19.2: For when you develop [*bhāvayato*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] of compassion [*karuṇā*], whatever harmfulness there is will be abandoned.
mn62:2.1: Then, in the morning, the *Bhagavā* robed himself and, taking bowl and robe, entered Sāvatthī for alms.
mn62:2.2: And Venerable Rāhula too, in the morning, robed himself and, taking bowl and robe, followed right behind the *Bhagavā*.
mn62:20.1: Develop [*bhāvehi*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] of gladness [*muditā*].
mn62:20.2: For when you develop [*bhāvayato*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] of gladness [*muditā*], whatever discontent there is will be abandoned.
mn62:21.1: Develop [*bhāvehi*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] of equanimity [*upekkhā*].
mn62:21.2: For when you develop [*bhāvayato*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] of equanimity [*upekkhā*], whatever aversion there is will be abandoned.
mn62:22.1: Develop [*bhāvehi*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] of the unattractive.
mn62:22.2: For when you develop [*bhāvayato*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] of the unattractive, whatever lust there is will be abandoned.
mn62:23.1: Develop [*bhāvehi*], Rāhula, the *saññā* of the impermanent [*aniccasaññaṁ*].
mn62:23.2: For when you develop [*bhāvayato*], Rāhula, the *saññā* of the impermanent [*aniccasaññā*], whatever the conceit ‘I am’ is will be abandoned.
mn62:24.1: Develop [*bhāvehi*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] of mindfulness of breathing.
mn62:24.2: For mindfulness of breathing, Rāhula, when developed [*bhāvitā*] and made much of, has great fruit and great benefit.
mn62:24.3: And how, Rāhula, is mindfulness of breathing, when developed [*bhāvitā*], of great fruit and great benefit? How, when made much of, is it of great fruit and great benefit?
mn62:25.1: Here, Rāhula, a *bhikkhu*, gone to the forest, or to the root of a tree, or to an empty hut, sits down, having folded his legs crosswise, set his body straight, and established *sati* in front of him.
mn62:25.2: With *sati* he breathes in; with *sati* he breathes out.
mn62:26.1: Breathing in long, he understands: ‘I breathe in long’; or breathing out long, he understands: ‘I breathe out long.’
mn62:26.2: Breathing in short, he understands: ‘I breathe in short’; or breathing out short, he understands: ‘I breathe out short.’
mn62:26.3: He trains thus: ‘Experiencing the whole body [*sabbakāyappaṭisaṁvedī*], I will breathe in’; he trains thus: ‘Experiencing the whole body [*sabbakāyappaṭisaṁvedī*], I will breathe out.’
mn62:26.4: He trains thus: ‘Calming the bodily *saṅkhāra* [*kāyasaṅkhāraṁ*], I will breathe in’; he trains thus: ‘Calming the bodily *saṅkhāra* [*kāyasaṅkhāraṁ*], I will breathe out.’
mn62:27.1: He trains thus: ‘Experiencing *pīti* [*pītippaṭisaṁvedī*], I will breathe in’; he trains thus: ‘Experiencing *pīti* [*pītippaṭisaṁvedī*], I will breathe out.’
mn62:27.2: He trains thus: ‘Experiencing *sukha* [*sukhappaṭisaṁvedī*], I will breathe in’; he trains thus: ‘Experiencing *sukha* [*sukhappaṭisaṁvedī*], I will breathe out.’
mn62:27.3: He trains thus: ‘Experiencing the *saṅkhāra* of mind [*cittasaṅkhārappaṭisaṁvedī*], I will breathe in’; he trains thus: ‘Experiencing the *saṅkhāra* of mind [*cittasaṅkhārappaṭisaṁvedī*], I will breathe out.’
mn62:27.4: He trains thus: ‘Calming the *saṅkhāra* of mind [*cittasaṅkhāraṁ*], I will breathe in’; he trains thus: ‘Calming the *saṅkhāra* of mind [*cittasaṅkhāraṁ*], I will breathe out.’
mn62:28.1: He trains thus: ‘Experiencing the mind [*cittappaṭisaṁvedī*], I will breathe in’; he trains thus: ‘Experiencing the mind [*cittappaṭisaṁvedī*], I will breathe out.’
mn62:28.2: He trains thus: ‘Gladdening the mind [*abhippamodayaṁ cittaṁ*], I will breathe in’; he trains thus: ‘Gladdening the mind [*abhippamodayaṁ cittaṁ*], I will breathe out.’
mn62:28.3: He trains thus: ‘Bringing the mind [*citta*] into *samādhi* [*samādahaṁ cittaṁ*], I will breathe in’; he trains thus: ‘Bringing the mind [*citta*] into *samādhi* [*samādahaṁ cittaṁ*], I will breathe out.’
mn62:28.4: He trains thus: ‘Liberating the mind [*vimocayaṁ cittaṁ*], I will breathe in’; he trains thus: ‘Liberating the mind [*vimocayaṁ cittaṁ*], I will breathe out.’
mn62:29.1: He trains thus: ‘Contemplating the impermanent [*aniccānupassī*], I will breathe in’; he trains thus: ‘Contemplating the impermanent [*aniccānupassī*], I will breathe out.’
mn62:29.2: He trains thus: ‘Contemplating fading away [*virāgānupassī*], I will breathe in’; he trains thus: ‘Contemplating fading away [*virāgānupassī*], I will breathe out.’
mn62:29.3: He trains thus: ‘Contemplating cessation [*nirodhānupassī*], I will breathe in’; he trains thus: ‘Contemplating cessation [*nirodhānupassī*], I will breathe out.’
mn62:29.4: He trains thus: ‘Contemplating relinquishment [*paṭinissaggānupassī*], I will breathe in’; he trains thus: ‘Contemplating relinquishment [*paṭinissaggānupassī*], I will breathe out.’
mn62:3.1: Then the *Bhagavā*, looking back, addressed Venerable Rāhula:
mn62:3.2: “Whatever *rūpa* there is, Rāhula—past, future, or present; internal or external; coarse or subtle; inferior or superior; far or near—all *rūpa* should be seen as it actually is with proper discernment [*sammappaññāya*] thus: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’”
mn62:3.4: “*Rūpa* too, Rāhula, *vedanā* too, Rāhula, *saññā* too, Rāhula, *saṅkhāras* too, Rāhula, consciousness [*viññāṇa*] too, Rāhula.”
mn62:30.1: Thus, Rāhula, mindfulness of breathing, when developed [*bhāvitā*] and made much of in this way, has great fruit and great benefit.
mn62:30.2: When mindfulness of breathing, Rāhula, has been developed [*bhāvitāya*] and made much of in this way, even those final breaths cease while known, not unknown.”
mn62:30.4: Satisfied, Venerable Rāhula delighted in what the *Bhagavā* said.
mn62:30.5: The second is finished: The Greater Advice to Rāhula.
mn62:4.1: Then Venerable Rāhula, thinking, “Who would enter the village for alms today after being directly advised by the *Bhagavā* face to face?” turned back from there and sat down at the root of a certain tree, having folded his legs crosswise, set his body straight, and established *sati* in front of him.
mn62:5.1: Venerable Sāriputta saw Venerable Rāhula sitting at the root of a certain tree, having folded his legs crosswise, set his body straight, and established *sati* in front of him.
mn62:5.2: Seeing him, he addressed Venerable Rāhula:
mn62:5.3: “Develop [*bhāvehi*], Rāhula, the development [*bhāvanaṁ*] of mindfulness of breathing.
mn62:5.4: For the development [*bhāvanā*] of mindfulness of breathing, Rāhula, when developed [*bhāvitā*] and made much of, has great fruit and great benefit.”
mn62:6.1: Then, in the evening, Venerable Rāhula rose from seclusion and went to the *Bhagavā*. Having approached, he paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side. Sitting to one side, Venerable Rāhula said this to the *Bhagavā*:
mn62:7.1: “How, bhante, is mindfulness of breathing, when developed [*bhāvitā*], of great fruit and great benefit? How, when made much of, is it of great fruit and great benefit?”
mn62:8.1: “Whatever, Rāhula, is internal, personal, hard, solid, and appropriated—namely:
mn62:8.2: head hairs, body hairs, nails, teeth, skin, flesh, sinews, bones, bone marrow, kidneys, heart, liver, diaphragm, spleen, lungs, intestines, mesentery, stomach contents, feces—or whatever else there is internally, personal, hard, solid, and appropriated—
mn62:8.3: this is called the internal earth element, Rāhula.
mn62:8.4: And whatever is the internal earth element and whatever is the external earth element—they are just the earth element.
mn62:8.5: That should be seen as it actually is with proper discernment [*sammappaññāya*] thus: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
mn62:8.6: Having seen this as it actually is with proper discernment [*sammappaññāya*], one becomes disenchanted with the earth element and makes the mind [*citta*] dispassionate toward the earth element.
mn62:9.1: And what, Rāhula, is the water element?
mn62:9.2: The water element may be internal, or it may be external.
mn62:9.3: And what, Rāhula, is the internal water element?
mn62:9.4: Whatever is internal, personal, water, watery, and appropriated—namely:
mn62:9.5: bile, phlegm, pus, blood, sweat, fat, tears, grease, spittle, snot, joint-fluid, urine—or whatever else there is internally, personal, water, watery, and appropriated—
mn62:9.6: this is called the internal water element, Rāhula.
mn62:9.7: And whatever is the internal water element and whatever is the external water element—they are just the water element.
mn62:9.8: That should be seen as it actually is with proper discernment [*sammappaññāya*] thus: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
mn62:9.9: Having seen this as it actually is with proper discernment [*sammappaññāya*], one becomes disenchanted with the water element and makes the mind [*citta*] dispassionate toward the water element.
